payload.reason: authelia crashloop ~2d10h; dragonfly Service endpoints empty; operator NetworkPolicy blocks operator from probing admin port :9999 to elect master
payload.summary: Restore master election by adding control-plane=controller-manager label to dragonfly-operator pod template

evidence_pre.authelia_fatal: redis connection error: dial tcp 10.43.135.100:6379: connect: connection refused
evidence_pre.np_probe_labeled_control-plane_controlle: Connection to 10.42.5.146 9999 port [tcp/*] succeeded! (proves label is the gate)

payload.fix: Add control-plane=controller-manager to the dragonfly-operator pod template labels via the bjw-s defaultPodOptions.labels in the HelmRelease. Flux reconciles, the operator pod gets the label, can probe :9999, elects a master, applies role=master label to one pod, the dragonfly Service endpoint populates, and authelia recovers.
payload.risk_notes: Medium because auth-affecting + adds a pod-spec label, but no API surface or RBAC changes. Operator restart is benign — the StatefulSet is healthy.
payload.rollback: git revert the helmrelease commit; flux reconciles back. Operator returns to the same (broken) state but cluster is no worse off than now.
payload.root_cause: dragonfly-operator (deployed via bjw-s app-template) emits a NetworkPolicy on the Dragonfly CR that allows port 9999 (admin port) ingress only from pods labeled control-plane=controller-manager. The bjw-s app-template gives the operator pod app.kubernetes.io/* labels but NOT that kubebuilder label, so the operator cannot reach :9999 on the dragonfly pods to query dataset-load status and elect a master. Result: no pod gets role=master, the dragonfly Service has no endpoints, authelia cannot connect to redis at 10.43.135.100:6379 and crashloops.

payload.reason: HA (and any version-pinned restore-test image) Renovate PRs never auto-merge
payload.summary: Add packageRule enabling branch-automerge for hack/restore-test/** so a version-pinned image there stops gating the combined PR

payload.change: Add a dedicated packageRule: matchDatasources docker, matchFileNames hack/restore-test/**, automerge true, automergeType branch, matchUpdateTypes [minor,patch,digest], minimumReleaseAge 1 day, ignoreTests true. Placed after the home-automation Helm automerge rule, before the mise rule. Does not affect the critical-paths disable rule (hack/restore-test not in that list). Major bumps (postgres 16->18, mosquitto 2->3) remain manual since major is excluded.
payload.rollback: git revert the single commit touching .renovaterc.json5
payload.root_cause: ghcr.io/home-operations/home-assistant is version-pinned in TWO files: kubernetes/main/apps/home-automation/home-assistant/app/helmrelease.yaml (covered by home-automation automerge rule) AND hack/restore-test/docker-compose.yaml:43 (covered by NO automerge rule). Renovate bundles both file edits into one branch (PR #79). The hack/ occurrence resolves automerge=false for a minor bump, so the whole PR resolves to automerge:false (footer: Automerge: Disabled by config). zigbee2mqtt/zwave-js-ui/node-red escape this because they are :latest in the fixture (untracked); home-assistant is exact-pinned so it gets gated.

Verified-from-source: confirmIfDepUpdated guard + Digest is not updated literal exist in lib/workers/repository/update/branch/auto-replace.ts on renovate main; PR #79 carried only docker-compose hunk; debug footer had type/minor only (no type/digest).

payload.reason: Electrician needs durable timestamped evidence of outdoor-light power-restore blips that survives the 7-day recorder purge and HA restarts
payload.summary: Observe-only outdoor power-blip logger automation + 3 helpers (counter/input_datetime/input_text)

payload.discriminator: Primary signature = from:'unavailable' -> 'on'. Normal ~21:00 dusk activation is a clean off->on from the Hue bridge (no preceding unavailable) and therefore does NOT match. Validated by: (a) trigger config reasoning, (b) live history of light.lichtzuil_linksvoor showing dusk=off->on while blips involve unavailable, (c) a real off->on toggle on one representative confirming no blip entry is created.
payload.durable_storage: All 3 helpers created through HA WS config APIs (counter/create etc.) NOT direct .storage writes (per ha-storage-ws-save memory) so running state is correct and no HA restart is needed. Recorder purge does not affect counter/input_datetime/input_text current values which persist in .storage and survive restart.
payload.evidence_artifacts: helper state deltas, persistent-notification text, trace run_id, the automation YAML block, discriminator toggle result
payload.observe_only: Automation contains NO light.turn_on/turn_off. Actions limited to counter.increment, input_datetime.set_datetime, input_text.set_value, persistent_notification.create, notify.mobile_app_s25_ultra_sander.
payload.rollback: 1) automation: restore /config/automations.yaml from timestamped backup then automation.reload. 2) helpers: counter/delete, input_datetime/delete, input_text/delete via WS using the returned ids. Backup path recorded in executed event.
payload.scope: 19 outdoor Hue lights = members of light.achtertuin group (lichtzuil x5, schutting x4, spot_boom x3, lamp_buitenkeuken, lichtslang_boom, lichtstrip_raam_overkapping, lichtstrip_tuinhuis x2, lichtstrip_buitenkeuken). Indoor Hue excluded.
payload.trigger_design: state trigger on 8 representative member lights, from:unavailable to:on. mode:single + 90s cooldown (delay at end) so one physical blip = one log entry, not 19. Group entity light.achtertuin NOT used as trigger (history proves the group aggregates and never records unavailable; only individual members record the unavailable power-restore signature).

validation_plan.test1_action_chain_e2e: Capture before-state of counter.outdoor_blip_count, input_datetime.outdoor_blip_last, input_text.outdoor_blip_log. Fire the automation action sequence (automation.trigger with skip_condition true). Capture after-state proving: counter incremented by 1, input_datetime updated to ~now, input_text appended newest-first. Read back the persistent_notification text via ha-cli.sh persistent. Record the trace run_id via ha-cli.sh trace.
validation_plan.test2_discriminator: Real off->on toggle on one representative (light.lamp_buitenkeuken): turn on, observe NO counter increment / NO new blip entry, then restore to off. Proves dusk-style off->on does not trigger.
validation_plan.test3_honest_limit: True unavailable->on power-restore cannot be force-induced (cannot make a Hue lamp go unavailable on demand). Documented: trigger uses from:unavailable to:on across 8 representatives; the next real blip will be captured. The Jun-7 04:10 flicker-burst variant that recorded NO unavailable is a secondary signature this automation will MISS by design (documented as follow-up).

external_actions: Created 3 helpers via WS config API (counter/create, input_datetime/create, input_text/create) directly in HA pod — counter.outdoor_blip_count, input_datetime.outdoor_blip_last, input_text.outdoor_blip_log; all returned success:true

test3_honest_limit.note1: True unavailable->on power-restore cannot be force-induced (cannot make a Hue lamp go unavailable on demand), so the real signature is proven only by trigger-config reasoning + the next actual blip. Trigger is state, from:'unavailable' to:'on' on 8 representative member lights.
test3_honest_limit.note2: GROUNDED in live recorder history: members (light.lichtzuil_linksvoor, light.schutting_voor) record uniques off/on/unavailable; the Hue GROUP light.achtertuin only records off/on (never unavailable) — hence the trigger uses individual members, not the group.
test3_honest_limit.note3_known_gap: The Jun-7 04:10 (02:10 UTC) blip was recorded as a rapid on/off/on/off burst with NO 'unavailable' state in the recorder. A pure from:'unavailable' trigger MISSES that variant. The Jun-9 03:05-style unavailable->on variant IS caught. Recommend a follow-up secondary trigger for the flicker-burst signature if the electrician needs every variant.
test3_honest_limit.note4_dropout_while_on: A routine Hue connectivity dropout while a light was already ON recovers unavailable->on and WOULD match. These are not power blips. Acceptable per user's explicit discriminator definition; flagged as possible minor over-count to review against the real first hits.
